title: daboecia cantabrica subsp. azorica
intro: Daboecia cantabrica subsp. azorica, also known as the Azores daboecia or heather of the Azores, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, derived from its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of daboecia cantabrica subsp. azorica include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. Daboecia cantabrica subsp. azorica is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies and homeopathy.
